outcome_text: The business value or context of business use has been clearly defined or—in the case of assessing existing AI systems—the ongoing relevance of the AI system to business requirements and its context of use has been evaluated.
scoring_text: Ability to evaluate AI system business value and relevance, including defining business use cases for new AI systems, assessing ongoing business alignment of existing AI systems, and conducting relevance evaluations against evolving business requirements and operational contexts.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate AI system business value and relevance, including defining business use cases for new AI systems, assessing ongoing business alignment of existing AI systems, and conducting relevance evaluations against evolving business requirements and operational contexts.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in evaluating AI use case feasibility, including data availability assessment, technical complexity estimation, and business value quantification.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-1.3 (MAP) equal WIDAI AI domain. AI-S-018 is the strongest match (STS: 0.699, cosine: 0.727). Secondary: LS-S-008 (LS, cosine: 0.648), DG-S-002 (DG, cosine: 0.646).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.0/10 (raw 0.699). Shared vocabulary: [ai, business, value]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [ability, against, alignment, assessing, cases, conducting] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, availability, case, complexity, data, estimation] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.901 (P=0.895, R=0.907). Balanced P/R — the 37-word competency implication and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.727 ≈ STS 0.699 (gap 0.028). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, business, value] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, business, value]. NIST AI RMF-only: [ability, against, alignment, assessing, cases, conducting]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, availability, case, complexity, data, estimation].
